text:
🔭 Enterprise workflow AI equilibrium

The "NL layer replaces SaaS UI" thesis is directionally supported but early. The equilibrium: SaaS becomes backend infrastructure, AI orchestrates on top. CRM/sales furthest along (Salesforce Agentforce: 18,500 customers, 3B monthly workflows); ERP/finance weakest evidence due to compliance & data gravity. Platform incumbents (SAP, Salesforce, Microsoft) are actively walling off third-party AI agents to own the orchestration layer.

Key points:
• Gartner: <5% enterprise agentic deployment in 2025 → 40% by 2026. IDC (~900 cos): 80%+ reconsidering packaged app investments. 70% of SaaS vendors will drop seat-based pricing by 2028.
• SAP API lockdown (April 29, enforcement June 9 2026) explicitly blocks third-party AI agents — yet only 3% of SAP customers use Joule vs 77% using Microsoft Copilot (just restricted). Salesforce and ServiceNow have not followed.
• Deterministic (n8n/Zapier/Make) wins when every step is known & stakes are low. Agents win when the workflow requires reasoning. Failure mode: applying agents to deterministic problems = cost+latency, no gain.
• Sequoia "copilot vs autopilot" (Julien Bek, Apr 2026): copilots sell tools, autopilots sell finished work. For every $1 on software, $6 on services — AI is going after services budget. Top verticals: staffing, legal transactional, IT managed services.
• a16z "process power": defensible moat = orchestration layer routing model output through domain-specific workflows, not the NL UI itself.
